title: Top 10 Games I'm Looking Forward to in 2020
body:
https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=JpfqYvUYNr8

Daniel (@dungeondive) returns with his most anticipated games for 2020, this time with the benefit of hindsight on his 2019 predictions. Dungeon Universalis made it through with its delays intact (those English translation errors!), Wander: Cult of Barnacle Bay actually released, and Hellboy proved to be a top-tier experience. The fresh list showcases a different flavor: Return to Dark Tower's technological marvel potential, the modular promise of Dungeons of Infinity, Waste Knights 2nd Edition's choose-your-own-adventure campaign, and Carnival Zombie's unique grotesque aesthetic. Daniel's particularly intrigued by Bloodborne the board game, hoping it captures the cosmic horror that made Cthulhu Death May Die such a pleasant surprise, and The Ghosts Betwixt from an online friend offers a fresh haunted house dungeon crawl theme.

But Daniel's real excitement peaks with Altar Quest's modular deck system (even if he wishes for standees instead of miniatures) and the ocean exploration dreams represented by Ever Rain and Sleeping Gods. Sleeping Gods, in particular, gets top billing—Daniel's hopeful that Ryan Lockey finally nails the marriage between Euro mechanics and thematic storytelling in a way that his previous designs haven't quite achieved.

----

Looking back now, which of these 2020 games ended up being keeper-table staples for Daniel, and did any of them falter after release in ways he didn't anticipate?
